meta.title: Constraint Engineering — The 3-Part Series
meta.description: Constraints as product. Eval harnesses as development loops. DNA contracts that ground autonomous agents.

props.title: Constraint Engineering
props.subtitle: The harness IS the development loop, not a bolt-on testing tool.

props.content: Context engineering taught us how to talk to models. Harness engineering taught us how to run them safely. Constraint engineering is the next floor — encoding behavioral contracts that grow stronger with every validation tick, until the gates enforce themselves. Prompts converge. Harnesses differentiate.

props.label: THE EVOLUTION
props.title: Why Constraints Are The Product
props.subtitle: Code is an artifact of DNA, not the other way around

props.content:
Three evolutionary layers:

**Context engineering** — the art of feeding the right information to the model.

**Harness engineering** — wrapping the model in a sandbox that catches its failures.

**Constraint engineering** — making the sandbox's gates the primary artifact. Instead of writing code and hoping tests catch regressions, you write the constraints that MUST hold and derive the code from them.

14/15 treatment vs 0/15 control on DNA-driven development across Claude and Gemini. Every core module at Leviathan ships with a .dna.yaml behavioral contract. Every contract has executable check scripts. Every check feeds the validator chain. The chain validates itself.

props.label: THE SERIES
props.title: Three Parts, One Spine
props.subtitle: From philosophy to implementation to battlefield proof

props.title: 01 — The Manifesto
props.content:
**PUBLISHED** — Constraints are the product. Specs explain them. Code implements them. Gates enforce them.

The foundational essay. Why the 4-step core (DNA → Graph → Execute → Validate) replaces traditional waterfall and test-driven development. How eval harnesses become the development loop itself.
The grounding principle: autonomous agents only stay autonomous if the constraints are immovable. [Read the Manifesto →](https://trusty-maple-wvcb.here.now/)

props.title: 02 — Consumer Lens
props.content:
**PUBLISHED** — What this means if you ship products to real humans.

How constraint engineering changes shipping velocity, reduces regression surface, and turns spec drift from a tax into a gate. Why your product team should care about behavioral contracts.

props.title: 03 — Browser Harness (Dogfood)
props.content:
**FORTHCOMING** — The case study. How we built the browser tool path through three wrong turns and ended up with a constraint contract that makes the right path the only path.

Includes: Substack extraction proof (24K markdown + 1080p video from a paid post, through Chrome APIs alone).

props.label: THE SPINE
props.title: What Every Part Returns To
props.subtitle: These five primitives show up in every article

props.title: 01 DNA
props.content: The behavioral contract. Constraints, behaviors, failure modes — executable YAML.

props.title: 02 Graph
props.content: The intent compiled into nodes. FlowMind is the graph materializer.

props.title: 03 Execute
props.content: Run the graph through poly (CLI, MCP, HTTP, gRPC, cross-language SDK).

props.title: 04 Validate
props.content: Every gate runs every tick. Adversarial + real-world, not unit tests.

props.title: 05 Harness
props.content: The development loop itself. The harness is the product, not a QA afterthought.

props.label: DEEP DIVES
props.title: 11 Articles
props.subtitle: Each explores one facet of constraint engineering

props.title: The Genesis Pattern
props.content: Four steps to AI-native software. DNA → constraints → code → validate.

props.title: Why Your AI Testing Is Broken
props.content: Eval harnesses are not test suites. The difference matters.

props.title: Eval Harnesses ≠ Test Suites
props.content: produce → judge → compare → keep or discard. Not assert.

props.title: The Adversarial Moat
props.content: How every exploit makes your system stronger. Gates as immune system.

props.title: From Prototype to Production in One Ratchet
props.content: The gate lifecycle. Constraints never demote (C4).

props.title: The Universal SDLC
props.content: Why this cycle works for everything, not just code.

props.title: Agent-Native CLI Design
props.content: Why MCP vs CLI is the wrong debate. Both are surfaces.

props.title: AgentFS: The Sandbox
props.content: Your AI agents can't escape. CoW overlay filesystem.

props.title: Skills That Learn
props.content: From markdown prompts to deterministic pipelines.

props.title: The Self-Documenting Runtime
props.content: When your --help IS your AI guide.

props.title: Why Two Teams Named It 'Ralph'
props.content: Convergent evolution proves the pattern is fundamental.

If you care about **HOW**: start with Part 1 (Manifesto). If you care about **WHY**: start with Part 2 (Consumer). If you want to see it **work on a real system**: start with Part 3 (Browser Harness). They converge on the same point — constraints are immovable, and that's the point.

Each article below stands alone. They all return to the same spine: DNA → Graph → Execute → Validate → Harness.
